title: Agency Debt Securities - Federal Reserve Bank of New York

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York, acting under FOMC directives, conducts purchases and sales of agency debt securities (obligations of Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and Federal Home Loan Banks) through the System Open Market Account (SOMA) with primary dealers. These transactions affect reserve balances in the banking system. Large-scale purchases occurred between 2009-2010 as part of monetary easing to pressure longer-term rates lower and support mortgage markets.
key_points: The New York Fed's Open Market Trading Desk executes agency debt transactions with primary dealers on behalf of the FOMC
Agency debt securities include direct obligations of Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and Federal Home Loan Banks
Purchases increase reserve balances in the banking system; sales or maturities reduce them
2009-2010 purchases were part of large-scale asset purchase programs combining agency debt with MBS purchases to ease monetary policy
The Federal Reserve previously purchased agency debt from 1971 through the early 1990s, and briefly purchased agency discount notes in 2008
The New York Fed provides agency debt transaction services to approximately 200 central banks, governments, and official institutions
why_it_matters: This describes the operational mechanics of how the Federal Reserve conducts open market operations in agency debt markets. Understanding the Fed's ability to purchase and sell agency securities, the counterparties involved (primary dealers), and how these transactions flow through the banking system (via reserve balances) is essential for comprehending U.S. monetary policy transmission and fixed income market structure.
macro_context: The source describes monetary policy transmission through open market operations. The 2008-2010 asset purchase programs (LSAPs) aimed to ease financial conditions by lowering longer-term interest rates and supporting mortgage markets following the financial crisis.
market_structure_context: The source describes the institutional structure of Fed open market operations, including: (1) the role of the New York Fed's Trading Desk as executor, (2) primary dealers as counterparties, (3) SOMA as the vehicle for holdings, and (4) the connection between Fed operations and the banking system's reserve balances. This defines the primary dealer market structure for Fed transactions.
